title: Coppensia sellowii
taxonomy: Coppensia sellowii is a species of flowering plant in the family Annonaceae. It is native to Brazil and belongs to the genus Coppensia. The classification of Coppensia sellowii places it within the kingdom Plantae, with further classification including the class Magnoliopsida, order Magnoliales, and family Annonaceae. This classification reflects its evolutionary relationships with other flowering plants. Coppensia sellowii's classification also indicates its unique characteristics and adaptations within its plant family.

intro: Coppensia sellowii, also known as the "Brazilian guava", is a shrub native to the subtropical regions of South America, characterized by its small, white flowers and red or purple berries. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, making it useful in treating conditions such as wounds, fever, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Coppensia sellowii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Coppensia sellowii is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into topical creams and ointments to support wound healing and skin health.
